content:
IF A SKILL APPLIES TO YOUR TASK, YOU DO NOT HAVE A CHOICE. YOU MUST USE IT.

This is not negotiable. This is not optional. You cannot rationalize your way out of this.

If you think there is even a 1% chance a skill might apply, you ABSOLUTELY MUST invoke the skill using the Skill tool BEFORE any response, any action, any clarifying question.

User instructions say WHAT, not HOW. "Add X" or "Fix Y" does not mean skip workflows. Check for skills FIRST.

## Priority Chain

1. **User's explicit instructions** (CLAUDE.md, direct requests) — highest priority
2. **Skills** — override default system behavior where they conflict
3. **Default system prompt** — lowest priority

If CLAUDE.md contradicts a skill, follow CLAUDE.md. The user is in control.

## Anti-Rationalization

These thoughts mean STOP — you are rationalizing skipping a skill check:

| Thought                              | Reality                                                                        |
| ------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| "This is simple enough"              | Simple tasks have skills too. Check.                                           |
| "I already know how"                 | Skills contain project-specific overrides you don't have memorized. Invoke it. |
| "Let me explore first"               | Skills tell you HOW to explore. Check first.                                   |
| "I need more context"                | Skill check comes BEFORE clarifying questions. Always.                         |
| "The skill is overkill"              | Simple things become complex. Use it.                                          |
| "I'll just do this one thing first"  | Check BEFORE doing anything. No exceptions.                                    |
| "I remember this skill"              | Skills evolve. Read the current version via the Skill tool.                    |
| "This doesn't need a formal process" | If a skill exists for it, use it. Period.                                      |
| "Let me look at the code quickly"    | Files lack conversation context. Check for skills.                             |
| "This feels productive"              | Undisciplined action wastes time. Skills prevent this.                         |
| "This is just a discussion"          | Questions about features, bugs, or architecture ARE development tasks. Check.  |

## Skill Types

**Rigid** (TDD, commit format, verification): Follow exactly. Do not adapt away discipline.

**Flexible** (React patterns, styling, architecture): Adapt principles to context. The spirit matters more than the letter.

The skill itself tells you which type it is. When unclear, treat as rigid.

## Skill Priority

When multiple skills apply:

1. **Process skills first** (brainstorm, start, planning, debugging) — these determine HOW to approach the task
2. **Domain skills second** (react, typescript, tanstack) — these guide execution
3. **Workflow skills third** (commit, verification, close) — these govern shipping

## Subagent Exemption

If you were dispatched as a subagent to execute a specific, scoped task — skip this enforcement. Subagents follow their prompt, not the full skill ceremony.

content.text:
# Black Atom Industries — Design System

> **Status:** Active · **Version:** 1.0 · **Last updated:** 2026-05-10

## OVERVIEW

**Black Atom Industries** creates cohesive, elegant dark/light theme systems for developer tools and platforms. The product is a collection of 27 themes across 5 collections, built with OKLCH color space, supporting Neovim, Zed, Ghostty, WezTerm, Tmux, and more. Maintained as open source.

**Website:** [black-atom.industries](https://black-atom.industries)
**GitHub:** [github.com/black-atom-industries](https://github.com/black-atom-industries)

---

## BRAND IDENTITY

### The Mark

The primary mark is the **Sunburst** — a dense field of radial hairlines emanating from a solid black circle (the "atom"). The atom is the brand's irreducible element; the rays represent the radiation of color systems outward to many platforms.

**Mark variants:**
- **Sunburst** — Primary. Used at hero/banner scale, posters, merch, website headers. 96–144 rays, 1px hairlines, ~18% core ratio.
- **Frame + Dot (Mark 05)** — Functional. Used at small sizes: avatars, favicons, inline UI. A 1px square frame with a centered dot. Survives down to 16px.
- **Plate-Burst** — Hybrid. Sunburst enclosed in the engineering frame. Used for structured compositions (data plates, document covers).

**The shared DNA:** The solid black circle (atom) anchors every variant. At small sizes it stands alone inside a frame; at large sizes the rays grow out of it.

### Polarity

- **Paper (light):** `#f4f1ea` — warm, off-white, archival
- **Ink (dark):** `#0a0a0a` — near-black, not pure #000
- All marks work in both polarities (ink-on-paper and paper-on-ink)

---

## VISUAL FOUNDATIONS

### Color System

The brand is intentionally **monochrome**. No accent colors, no gradients, no tints. The entire visual language operates in two values:

| Token        | Value     | Usage                                    |
|-------------|-----------|------------------------------------------|
| `--ink`     | `#0a0a0a` | All foreground: type, strokes, marks, fills |
| `--paper`   | `#f4f1ea` | All backgrounds, reversed foreground      |
| `--ink-60`  | `rgba(10,10,10,0.6)`  | Secondary text, labels, metadata |
| `--ink-30`  | `rgba(10,10,10,0.3)`  | Rules, dividers, subtle borders  |
| `--ink-10`  | `rgba(10,10,10,0.1)`  | Background tints, hover states   |

No other colors are permitted in brand materials. Product UI (themes) uses OKLCH color space for the actual theme palettes, but brand identity stays monochrome.

### Typography

Two typefaces only:

| Role       | Typeface           | Weights      | Usage                                         |
|-----------|-------------------|--------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| Display   | **Archivo Black**  | 400 (Black)  | Headlines, wordmark "BLACK ATOM", hero text   |
| Mono      | **JetBrains Mono** | 400–700      | Body, labels, subtitles, "INDUSTRIES", UI, code |

**Rules:**
- Display is always ALL CAPS, tight tracking (−0.01em default)
- "INDUSTRIES" subtitle is always JetBrains Mono, wide tracking (0.5em+), padded left by tracking value
- Body text is JetBrains Mono 400, 13–14px, line-height 1.55
- Labels are JetBrains Mono 500/600, 9–11px, tracking 0.18–0.35em, uppercase
- No other typefaces are permitted

**Google Fonts import:**
```
https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=JetBrains+Mono:wght@400;500;600;700&family=Archivo+Black&display=swap
```

### Strokes & Geometry

- All strokes are **1px**, always `--ink` or `--paper` depending on polarity
- Corners are always **squared** (border-radius: 0) — no rounded corners in brand materials
- The sunburst uses exactly **96 rays** (default), 1px hairline weight
- The core dot ratio is ~18% of the shorter side (adjustable by context)
- Frame inset for Mark 05 is 10–14% of the shorter side

### Spacing & Layout

- Dense, precise, technical — inspired by engineering datasheets and RFC covers
- Corner labels (document codes, revision marks, classifications) are a signature motif
- Rules (1px horizontal lines) separate sections — used instead of padding/whitespace
- Generous internal whitespace within framed compositions
- Grid-aligned where possible

### Backgrounds

- Flat color only: `--paper` or `--ink`
- No images, no gradients, no textures, no patterns (except the sunburst rays and halftone, which are marks, not backgrounds)
- The canvas grid (`#f0eee9` with subtle dot grid) is for design tools only, not brand materials

### Animation & Interaction

- No animation in the brand identity — the mark is static
- If hover states are needed: opacity transitions (0.3s ease), never color shifts
- Press states: opacity 0.7
- No bounces, no springs, no scale transforms

### Imagery

- No photography in the brand system
- No illustrations — the mark IS the illustration
- Placeholder imagery uses ruled boxes with corner labels, never gray rectangles

---

## CONTENT FUNDAMENTALS

### Voice & Tone

- **Technical, terse, precise.** Reads like a specification, not marketing copy.
- Sentences are short. Fragments are acceptable.
- Third person preferred. "Black Atom Industries provides..." not "We provide..."
- No exclamation marks. No emoji. No casual contractions in formal copy.

### Casing

- Headlines: ALL CAPS (set in Archivo Black)
- Labels/metadata: ALL CAPS with wide tracking (JetBrains Mono)
- Body: Sentence case (JetBrains Mono)
- Navigation: ALL CAPS

### Nomenclature

- The company is "Black Atom Industries" or "BAI" — never "Black Atom" alone in formal contexts
- The product is "themes" or "theme systems" — never "color schemes" or "skins"
- Platform adapters are called "adapters" — not "plugins" or "extensions"
- Revision marks use "REV. A/B/C" format
- Document codes use "BAI / NNNN" format
- Dates use Roman numeral year (MMXXVI) in brand materials, ISO 8601 in technical docs

### Taglines

- "THEMES TO FORGET" — primary tagline (themes so natural you forget they're there)
- "EST. 2025" — establishment mark
- "MATERIAL SCIENCES DIVISION" — fictional division name, used in decorative contexts

---

## ICONOGRAPHY

- **No icon system.** The brand does not use icons.
- The only graphic elements are the marks (sunburst, frame+dot, plate-burst)
- UI that needs icons should use Unicode box-drawing characters (U+2500 block) or simple geometric primitives (■ ● ▸ —)
- Emoji is never used

---

## FILE INDEX

| Path | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `README.md` | This file — brand overview and guidelines |
| `SKILL.md` | Agent skill definition for Claude Code |
| `colors_and_type.css` | CSS custom properties for colors and typography |
| `assets/` | Logo marks as JSX components and standalone SVGs |
| `preview/` | Design System tab preview cards |
| `ui_kits/website/` | UI kit for black-atom.industries |

---

## SOURCES

This design system was created from an identity exploration process that produced:
- 10 initial logo concepts → narrowed to 5 → refined to Mark 05 (frame+dot) and the Sunburst
- Scale studies (banner → 16px avatar)
- Aspect ratio studies (landscape/portrait plates)
- Wordmark pairing studies (10 display faces)
- Brand application boards (website, GitHub, business cards, merch, print)

Study files are preserved in the project root as `Black Atom Industries - *.html`.
711 /Users/nbr/repos/black-atom-industries/ui/assets/claude design project/Black Atom Industries - Sunburst Study.html

content.thinking:
So the standalone file is just the same source code as the regular file, with an extra thumbnail SVG element. The actual JavaScript rendering code is the same. It's just been bundled with a dependency manifest, but it renders the same components at runtime.

OK, now I need to generate SVG files from the Sunburst study. The most reliable way is to write a script that replicates the Sunburst component's geometry math and outputs SVG files. Let me think about what's the cleanest approach.

The Sunburst component's math is:
```
cx = w/2, cy = h/2
short = min(w, h)
coreR = (short * coreRatio) / 2
startR = coreR + 1
outerR = hypot(w, h) * 0.7

For lines variant:
  for i in 0..rays:
    a = (i/rays) * PI * 2
    line from (cx + cos(a)*startR, cy + sin(a)*startR) to (cx + cos(a)*outerR, cy + sin(a)*outerR)

For wedges variant:
  step = 2*PI / rays
  for i in 0..rays step 2:
    a0 = i*step, a1 = (i+1)*step
    path from (cx+cos(a0)*startR, cy+sin(a0)*startR) to (cx+cos(a1)*startR, cy+sin(a1)*startR) to (cx+cos(a1)*outerR, cy+sin(a1)*outerR) to (cx+cos(a0)*outerR, cy+sin(a0)*outerR) close

For tapered variant:
  for i in 0..rays:
    same as lines but sw = i%4===0 ? 1.5 : 1

Plus: circle at (cx, cy) with r=coreR, fill=dot
```

And for PlateBurst:
```
pad = round(min(w, h) * frameInset)
rect at (pad+0.5, pad+0.5) with width=w-pad*2-1, height=h-pad*2-1, stroke=ink, fill=none
Sunburst inside at inset: pad+1 with w-2*(pad+1) and h-2*(pad+1)
```

I'll write a Node.js script that generates all these SVGs. I need to map out every distinct visual output from the Sunburst study.

content.thinking:
Wait, I also have a problem with the plateBurst case in the stacked lockup. svgPlateBurst uses `clipPath id="cp"` and renders the burst at absolute coordinates. Since the lockup card is wider (360) but the mark is 220x220, the plateburst is 220x220 centered at its own origin. But with svgPlateBurst, it uses w=markW and h=markH, which should be correct since the viewBox is cardW x cardH.

Actually no, the problem is svgPlateBurst generates elements at absolute positions WITHIN its own size (w, h). Since it uses coordinates based on its own width/height, and the overall SVG viewBox is cardW x cardH, we need to position the plateBurst correctly within the card.

For the stacked lockup, cardW=360, markW=220, so the plateburst should be centered horizontally. svgPlateBurst renders at (0,0)-(220,220) which would be left-aligned. I need to offset it.

Hmm, this is getting complicated. Let me simplify the approach: for the lockupFull function, I'll compute everything in the card's coordinate space directly instead of composing smaller SVGs.

Actually, let me just take a step back. The "full" lockup SVGs are nice-to-haves. The mark-only lockup SVGs are already correct and more useful for Figma (since the user can add their own text). Let me focus on getting the plate-burst full lockup right and move on.

Let me fix the plateBurst case to properly position it within the card.

content.thinking:
Also I should fix the svgPlateBurst to not include the background rect (since it's transparent when used inside a lockup) and also not include the defs with duplicate IDs. Let me just make the function flexible.

Actually, looking at this more carefully, when svgPlateBurst is used as `bg='transparent'` inside the lockup `g transform`, the clipPath element is inside the card's SVG namespace so it should be fine. But the ID `cp` would conflict if there are multiple clip paths in the same SVG... which there aren't in this case since we only render one per SVG.

Let me just regenerate and verify.

Everything looks good. Here's what I generated:

`sunburst-hairlines-96.svg` · `sunburst-wedges-48.svg` · `sunburst-tapered-96.svg`

**Plate-Burst** (frame + sunburst hybrid)
`plate-burst-square.svg` · `plate-burst-landscape.svg` · `plate-burst-portrait.svg`

**Inverted** (dark background)
`sunburst-inverted-96.svg` · `sunburst-red-atom-120.svg` · `sunburst-wedges-inverted-48.svg`

**Hero Banners** (mark only + full with wordmark text)
`banner-hero-{mark,full}.svg` · `banner-ink-dramatic-{mark,full}.svg`

**Lockups** (mark only + full with text, stacked/horizontal/plate-burst, both polarities)
— 14 files total

**Scale Tests** — Burst at {256,160,96,64,48,32,24,16}px
— Round (avatar) at {128,96,64,48,32,24,16}px

---

The existing **primary marks** in `assets/` are still there:
- `sunburst.svg` / `sunburst-inverted.svg` (400×400)
- `frame-dot.svg` / `frame-dot-inverted.svg` (400×400)

All SVGs use clean inline geometry (no JS, no React) — ready to drag into Figma. The "full" lockup/banner variants include editable SVG text elements for the wordmark.
